title: Cienfuegosia punctata
intro: Cienfuegosia punctata, also known as the punctate cienfuegosia or prickly poppy, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Cienfuegosia punctata include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cienfuegosia punctata is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and expectorant remedies to relieve respiratory issues.
